user.title: Load Need Predictor
user.description: Set when to predict (after tomorrow's prices publish) and when to capture the day's actual delivery.

data_description.predict_time: Time of day to compute the prediction and push it to the scheduler target — set it just after tomorrow's day-ahead prices publish.
data_description.capture_time: Time of day to record the day's actual delivered energy as a training sample (typically end of day).

data_description.predict_time: Time of day to compute the prediction and push it to the scheduler target — set it just after tomorrow's day-ahead prices publish.
data_description.capture_time: Time of day to record the day's actual delivered energy as a training sample (typically end of day).

init.title: Load
init.description: Pick the scheduler target to drive, the delivered-energy sensor (training signal), the occupancy entities, and the runtime clamp. Temperature/water sensors are logged for a future model, not used in the prediction.

data_description.target_number_entity: The Load Scheduler target number this predictor drives (e.g. number.water_heater_lvv_target_runtime). The prediction is written here.
data_description.delivered_energy_entity: Energy delivered to the load (kWh, total/increasing). Its day-to-day change is the training signal.
data_description.delivered_runtime_entity: Optional run-time sensor; used as the delivered signal instead of energy ÷ rated power when run-time is the better measure.
data_description.controlled_switch_entity: The load's switch/contactor — the relay the scheduler drives. Its recorded on-time (from any source: scheduler, a manual boost, an automation) measures the runtime actually delivered, so a day the scheduler skips on price/solar is carried over and made up the next day. Pick the real contactor, not a thermostat-gated power sensor. Leave empty to disable catch-up.
data_description.rated_power_kw: The load's power draw, used to convert predicted energy (kWh) into a run-time (minutes) for the scheduler target.
data_description.person_entities: People whose presence shifts demand. Counted when home for at least 12 h of the last 24 h, so a daytime absence (e.g. a meeting) doesn't drop them.
data_description.guests_calendar_entity: Calendar marking guest visits. Demand is weighted by visit length — a short visit (< 6 h) counts as ~half a person, a long one (≥ 6 h, likely sauna + showers) as two.
data_description.supply_temp_entity: Incoming mains-water temperature. Logged for a future model; not used in the current prediction.
data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Outdoor temperature. Logged for a future model; not used in the current prediction.
data_description.water_total_entity: Household water-meter total. Logged for a future model; not used in the current prediction.
data_description.min_minutes: Lower clamp on the pushed target — a safety floor so the load never starves.
data_description.max_minutes: Upper clamp on the pushed target — a cap so a bad prediction can't over-run the load.
data_description.deficit_cap_minutes: Largest backlog the catch-up can accumulate (minutes). Defaults to twice the daily cap — enough to recover a couple of skipped days without runaway after a long outage. Only used when a controlled switch is set.

init.title: Beyond-horizon price forecast
init.description: Estimate electricity prices beyond Nord Pool's day-ahead horizon from wind + temperature, and publish them for the scheduler's forecast_price_entity. The model is fit on long-term statistics of the price/temperature/wind history.

data_description.price_entity: Your real all-in buy-price sensor (€/kWh). The model is fit on its history and its accuracy is evaluated against it.
data_description.wind_entity: Finland wind-production forecast sensor — more wind generally means lower prices.
data_description.weather_entity: Weather entity providing the daily temperature forecast — colder means higher prices, especially in deep cold.
data_description.temp_history_entity: Outdoor temperature sensor whose recorded history is used to fit the price model.
data_description.forecast_days: How many days ahead to publish the price estimate (beyond Nord Pool's day-ahead horizon).
data_description.fit_days: How many days of history to fit the price model on.
